chapter_title.ar: باب فِيمَا يَأْخُذُ السُّلْطَانُ مِنَ الْخَرَاجِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 25 – Regarding what the Sultan (ruling authority) takes from the taxes

text: مَا أَخَذَهُ مِنْكَ الْعَاشِرُ فَطَرَحَهُ فِي كُوزَةٍ فَهُوَ مِنْ زَكَاتِكَ وَ مَا لَمْ يَطْرَحْ فِي الْكُوزِ فَلَا تَحْتَسِبْهُ مِنْ زَكَاتِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Ja’farasws, from hisasws forefathersasws having said: ‘Whatever the tax collector (of 10%) takes from you and throws it into a jug, so it is from your Zakāt, and whatever he does not throw in into the jug, so you cannot reckon it to be from your Zakāt’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has narrated from his ancestor, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, this Hadith. ‘Whatever the collector of one out of ten (Zakat collector) charges you and deposits it in the mug (the safe), it is your Zakat. If he does not do so then do not count it as your Zakat.’”
